flashcards.back: The set of terminals that begin strings derived from X, including ε if X can derive the empty string.
The set of terminals that can appear immediately to the right of non-terminal A in some sentential form.

flashcards.front: What symbol is always in FOLLOW(S) where S is the start symbol?

flashcards.back: No, FOLLOW sets only contain terminal symbols, never ε.

flashcards.front: When computing FIRST(αβ), what is the rule if α can derive ε?
flashcards.back: If α can derive ε, then FIRST(αβ) includes both FIRST(α) and FIRST(β).
